acceptance.criterion: Valid bounded TODO and checkpoint artifacts pass, while malformed IDs, missing validation, limits, multiple active items, identity mismatches, and runtime-root escapes fail with stable codes.
acceptance.evidence: Deterministic prewalk artifact and path tests pass.

acceptance.criterion: Model-free transition validation accepts task-relevant source, test-first, or product-documentation edits and rejects empty, staging-only, forbidden, outside-worktree, branch, origin, tag, or protected-ref changes.

Routing reports requested and actual prewalk mode, distinct guide/execution routes, advertised versus behavioral capability facts, instruction fidelity, provenance, fallback, and zero qualification model calls.
acceptance.evidence: Route matrix and read-only probes pass with zero model calls.

acceptance.criterion: `worker.prewalk` accepts only `off`, `auto`, or `required`, preserves existing safe preference precedence, grants no authority, and leaves unqualified auto runs on the backward-compatible single-phase route.
acceptance.evidence: Preference and route tests cover all modes and unqualified fallback.

acceptance.criterion: A fixture run traverses staged, guide, transition, execution, and complete states; sends the packet once; resumes with only `Continue.`; retains one exact session/worktree; and exposes both phases in one private follow log.

Missing or malformed TODO/checkpoint, session/worktree mismatch, forbidden edits, guide failure, resume failure, and post-edit cold fallback fail closed with stable actionable state while preserving the worktree and logs.

Codex uses exact `codex exec resume <session-id>` and Claude uses exact `--resume <uuid>` with explicit guide/execution model and effort, registered CWD/write roots, no ambiguous selector, and no packet replay.
acceptance.evidence: Codex and Claude command grammar parity tests pass.

acceptance.criterion: Tiny all-complete tasks may terminalize after the guide only when the task-complete checkpoint and normal completion/Git contracts hold; otherwise a zero guide exit means transition-ready, not complete.
acceptance.evidence: Atomic task fixture passes and incomplete artifacts fail.

acceptance.criterion: Existing version-2 and single-phase native-worker launch, status, follow, redaction, environment scrubbing, sandboxing, PID identity, Git authority, and no-push tests remain green.
acceptance.evidence: Existing native-worker lifecycle regression tests pass.

acceptance.criterion: Canonical and user-facing docs define prewalk only as exact-session guide-to-execution trajectory continuity, explicitly reject cold handoffs, preserve driver authority, and give equivalent valid Codex/Claude operations.
acceptance.evidence: Canonical, parity, README, and guide surfaces agree.

acceptance.criterion: Documentation reports retained-safe versus pruned instruction fidelity honestly, states that static help is not behavioral proof, and keeps automatic rollout disabled or conservative until both hosts are live-qualified.
acceptance.evidence: Normative capability and fidelity documentation is present.

acceptance.criterion: Fresh installed Codex and Claude bundles contain the prewalk runtime/reference and pass smoke checks without source-checkout-only helper dependencies.
Focused suites, repository consistency, `git diff --check`, and `python3 scripts/verify_repo.py --version Unreleased` pass on the reviewed exact tip.
acceptance.evidence: Focused and canonical verification passed before release promotion.

acceptance.criterion: The new PR is open against `main`, unmerged, and its description explains the old cold-handoff gap, implemented exact-session mechanism, parity evidence, pruning fidelity, verification, and remaining live qualification/rollout limits.
acceptance.evidence: PR #80 is open with the required description.

acceptance.criterion: `SKILL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, README, guide, changelog, and durable current-version examples consistently identify `2.8.0`; `Unreleased` is empty and the release checklist passes cleanly.

Codex and Claude documentation and deterministic fixtures preserve equivalent exact-ID, same-worktree, packet-once, checkpoint, recovery, visibility, authority, and retained-safe-only activation semantics without claiming a live qualification occurred.

Focused parity/release tests, both installed-bundle smokes, strict CI verification, final-readiness verification, the Elves landing check, PR comments/reviews, and required checks are green on the exact pre-merge tip.

continuation_guard.next_action: Promote and verify the v2.8.0 release documentation and parity contract, then land PR #80 and publish the GitHub release.

master_acceptance.criterion: One supervised native-worker run can begin on an explicit guide route, create a bounded validation-bearing TODO, make a meaningful edit, and automatically resume the exact same provider session and worktree on an explicit execution route with only `Continue.`.
The packet is sent exactly once; fresh sessions, copied transcripts, progress-note handoffs, ambiguous resume selectors, and post-edit cold fallback are never accepted or described as prewalk.
master_acceptance.evidence: Packet-once, selector, and fallback fixtures pass.

master_acceptance.criterion: Codex and Claude provide equivalent state, route, TODO/checkpoint, follow, recovery, failure, installation, and documentation semantics with fixture-backed tests and honest behavioral-qualification limits.
master_acceptance.evidence: Cross-host tables, fixtures, and bundle smokes pass before promotion.

master_acceptance.criterion: Existing single-phase workers and all environment, redaction, sandbox, Git-authority, protected-ref, PR, merge, and terminal-review contracts remain intact.
master_acceptance.evidence: Existing regression and repository verification gates pass.

master_acceptance.criterion: The exact final tip passes focused and canonical verification, cumulative review finds no unresolved serious issue, and an unmerged PR is ready for human review.
master_acceptance.evidence: PR #80 reached green reviewed readiness before the authorized release extension.
